policy_statement: TMG will strengthen evacuation support systems (including guidelines considering emerging infectious diseases and vulnerable groups), consider wide-area evacuation for large floods, expand shelter options including hotels/inns, update hazard maps, enhance flood measures for underground spaces, review/designate landslide-risk areas and support municipal alert/evacuation systems, and digitize disaster preparedness (digital signage, smart poles, open APIs).
mechanism_description: The policy strengthens evacuation support and shelter coordination, promotes updated hazard maps and flood/landslide measures, and digitizes disaster information systems to improve preparedness and reduce disaster risk impacts.
primary_causal_pathway: Climate hazard event → improved preparedness/response capacity → ↓ exposure & vulnerability → ↓ disaster impacts
causal_mechanism_detail: (1) Changes the governance and emergency-management system by strengthening evacuation support (including for vulnerable groups), expanding/organizing shelter options, updating hazard maps and risk designations, improving alert/evacuation protocols, and digitizing disaster information systems. (2) These measures improve risk identification, early warning dissemination, evacuation decision-making, and sheltering capacity/coordination during floods/landslides and other extremes. (3) Outcome is reduced human and economic impacts from climate-related hazards (lower mortality/morbidity, displacement, and damages), i.e., higher institutional adaptive capacity.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reducing loss of life and damages during climate-amplified disasters by improving preparedness, warning, evacuation, and shelter coordination.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: climate hazard occurrence → better preparedness/evacuation/shelter coordination and information → reduced exposure and vulnerability → reduced disaster impacts.  
2) Why Adaptation over alternatives: the core intent and effect is managing climate risk impacts (preparedness/response), not reducing emissions (Mitigation), reducing resource inputs (Resource Efficiency), or leveraging ecosystems (Nature-Based Solutions).  
3) Edge cases applied: some components (e.g., flood measures for underground spaces) can resemble physical resilience investments, but they still function primarily to reduce hazard impacts; digitization is an enabling measure whose climate relevance is through improved response capacity, not energy savings.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances sharing this mechanism: any policy that strengthens evacuation support, shelter coordination, hazard information, and disaster information systems primarily increases adaptive capacity and reduces disaster impacts.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment (e.g., flooding vs. landslide focus, public health co-benefits, critical infrastructure resilience) is deferred to policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If a specific implementation is primarily justified or designed for non-climate hazards (e.g., earthquakes) with minimal linkage to climate-exacerbated extremes, climate-adaptation relevance could weaken; however, the described elements (flood/landslide maps and measures) strongly indicate climate-risk adaptation.
key_indicators: "Strengthen the support system"; "revision of the evacuation center management and operation guidelines"; "wide-area evacuation measures"; "utilization of hotels and inns"; "update of hazard maps"; "enhancement of measures against inundation in underground spaces"; "review the designation of specific areas"; "provide technical support"; "digitization of disaster preparedness"; "digital signage"; "smart poles"; "open APIs"
co_benefits: Public health protection during evacuations (emerging infectious diseases); improved general disaster readiness and public information services beyond climate-specific events.
instance_edge_case_notes: Some elements could also be coded as infrastructure-investment (e.g., flood measures in underground spaces; digital signage/smart poles) and information-disclosure (hazard maps), but the bundle is primarily an integrated preparedness/evacuation planning and coordination package, so planning-strategy is most representative.
